message.content:
Below is an exhaustive, **test‑oriented checklist** of Open Graph Protocol (OGP) metadata based strictly on **ogp.me**. Each item includes:

- **Property / check**
- **What it verifies**
- **Exact ogp.me URL or section** (path/anchor as shown on ogp.me)

---

## 1. Global / Structural Checks

### 1.1 Correct use of `<meta property>` and placement in `<head>`

- **Check:** All Open Graph tags use the HTML `<meta>` element, with the **`property`** attribute (not `name`) and a `content` value, e.g.  
  `\<meta property="og:title" content="The Rock" /\>`.[1]  
  **Verifies:** Proper OGP syntax (property/content pattern).  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#types` (“This is done using the `og:type` property…”, example markup).

- **Check:** All Open Graph `<meta>` tags are placed **inside the HTML `<head>`** element.[1][3]  
  **Verifies:** Tags are in the correct document location for consumption by parsers.  
  **ogp.me section:** Example markup in `https://ogp.me/#types` shows tags in `<head>`.

---

## 2. Core Required Properties (Basic Metadata)

ogp.me defines **four required properties for every page**:[1]

### 2.1 `og:title`

- **Property / check:** `og:title` is present exactly once per logical object, e.g.:  
  `\<meta property="og:title" content="The Rock" /\>`.[1]  
  **Verifies:** The **title of the object** as it should appear within the graph.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#basic`

### 2.2 `og:type`

- **Property / check:** `og:type` is present and set to a supported type (e.g. `website`, `article`, `video.movie`, etc.).[1][4]  
  **Verifies:** The **type of the object**; determines which additional type‑specific properties may be required.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#basic` and type listing under `https://ogp.me/#types` and object examples.

### 2.3 `og:image`

- **Property / check:** `og:image` is present and set to a resolvable URL for an image that represents the object.[1]  
  **Verifies:** **Image URL** representing the object in the graph.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#basic` and image structured properties in `https://ogp.me/#structured`

### 2.4 `og:url`

- **Property / check:** `og:url` is present and set to the canonical, permanent URL for the object.[1]  
  **Verifies:** **Canonical URL** used as the object’s permanent ID in the graph.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#basic`

---

## 3. Optional Global Properties (Recommended for Any Object)

From ogp.me “The following properties are optional for any object and are generally recommended”:[1]

### 3.1 `og:description`

- **Property / check:** `og:description` present and non‑empty.[1]  
  **Verifies:** A **one to two sentence description** of the object.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#optional`

### 3.2 `og:site_name`

- **Property / check:** `og:site_name` present when the object is part of a larger website.[1]  
  **Verifies:** The **name of the overall site** (e.g., “IMDb”).  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#optional`

### 3.3 `og:locale`

- **Property / check:** `og:locale` present and formatted as `language_TERRITORY` (e.g., `en_US`).[1]  
  **Verifies:** The **locale** the tags are marked up in; default is `en_US` if omitted.[1]  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#optional`

### 3.4 `og:locale:alternate`

- **Property / check:** Zero or more `og:locale:alternate` tags may be present, each with a `language_TERRITORY` value.[1]  
  **Verifies:** **Additional locales** in which the page is available.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#optional`

### 3.5 `og:audio`

- **Property / check:** `og:audio` present only if an audio file accompanies the object.[1]  
  **Verifies:** URL of an **audio file** to accompany the object.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#optional` and audio structured properties in `https://ogp.me/#structured`

### 3.6 `og:video`

- **Property / check:** `og:video` present only if a video file accompanies the object.[1]  
  **Verifies:** URL of a **video file** that complements the object.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#optional` and video structured properties in `https://ogp.me/#structured`

### 3.7 `og:determiner`

- **Property / check:** If present, `og:determiner` value is one of: `a`, `an`, `the`, `""` (empty string), or `auto`.[1]  
  **Verifies:** The **word that appears before the object’s title** in a sentence; if `auto`, the consumer chooses `a` or `an`; default is blank.[1]  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#optional`

---

## 4. Structured Properties for Images

ogp.me defines structured properties for `og:image`:[1]

### 4.1 `og:image:url`

- **Property / check:** If used, `og:image:url` duplicates the primary image URL; `og:image` itself may also be present.[1]  
  **Verifies:** Explicit **image URL** (identical to `og:image`).  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#structured`

### 4.2 `og:image:secure_url`

- **Property / check:** If present, `og:image:secure_url` is an HTTPS URL for the image.[1]  
  **Verifies:** Alternate **HTTPS image URL** for secure contexts.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#structured`

### 4.3 `og:image:type`

- **Property / check:** If present, `og:image:type` is a valid MIME type (e.g., `image/jpeg`).[1]  
  **Verifies:** **MIME type** of the image.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#structured`

### 4.4 `og:image:width`

- **Property / check:** If present, `og:image:width` is a positive integer.[1]  
  **Verifies:** **Width in pixels** of the image.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#structured`

### 4.5 `og:image:height`

- **Property / check:** If present, `og:image:height` is a positive integer.[1]  
  **Verifies:** **Height in pixels** of the image.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#structured`

### 4.6 `og:image:alt`

- **Property / check:** If an `og:image` is specified, check whether `og:image:alt` is also provided and non‑empty.[1]  
  **Verifies:** **Text description of the image** (not a caption). ogp.me states: “If the page specifies an og:image it should specify `og:image:alt`.”[1]  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#structured`

---

## 5. Structured Properties for Audio (for completeness)

While you didn’t list these explicitly, ogp.me defines structured audio properties that are relevant for testing when `og:audio` is used:[1]

### 5.1 `og:audio:url`

- **Property / check:** If used, `og:audio:url` is identical to `og:audio`.[1]  
  **Verifies:** Explicit **audio URL**.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#structured`

### 5.2 `og:audio:secure_url`

- **Property / check:** If present, `og:audio:secure_url` is an HTTPS URL.[1]  
  **Verifies:** **Secure audio URL**.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#structured`

### 5.3 `og:audio:type`

- **Property / check:** If present, `og:audio:type` is a valid MIME type (e.g., `audio/mpeg`).[1]  
  **Verifies:** **MIME type** of the audio.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#structured`

---

## 6. Structured Properties for Video (for completeness)

ogp.me also defines parallel structured properties for `og:video`:[1]

### 6.1 `og:video:url`

- **Property / check:** If used, `og:video:url` is identical to `og:video`.[1]  
  **Verifies:** Explicit **video URL**.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#structured`

### 6.2 `og:video:secure_url`

- **Property / check:** If present, `og:video:secure_url` is an HTTPS URL.[1]  
  **Verifies:** **Secure video URL**.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#structured`

### 6.3 `og:video:type`

- **Property / check:** If present, `og:video:type` is a valid MIME type (e.g., `video/mp4`).[1]  
  **Verifies:** **MIME type** of the video.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#structured`

### 6.4 `og:video:width`

- **Property / check:** If present, `og:video:width` is a positive integer.[1]  
  **Verifies:** **Width in pixels** of the video.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#structured`

### 6.5 `og:video:height`

- **Property / check:** If present, `og:video:height` is a positive integer.[1]  
  **Verifies:** **Height in pixels** of the video.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#structured`

---

## 7. Type‑Specific Objects and Properties

ogp.me defines a number of **object types**, each with its own type‑specific properties.[1]  
All type definitions and examples live under `https://ogp.me/#types` (and linked sub‑sections).

### 7.1 Article (`article`)

- **Check:** `og:type` is `article` for news articles or blog posts that “have a URL and title”.[1]  
  **Verifies:** Correct classification as an **article** object.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#type_article`

Type‑specific article properties (for completeness):

- `article:published_time` – When the article was first published.[1]  
- `article:modified_time` – When the article was last changed.[1]  
- `article:expiration_time` – When it stops being relevant.[1]  
- `article:author` – URL of author profile(s).[1]  
- `article:section` – High‑level section (e.g., “Technology”).[1]  
- `article:tag` – Tag words associated with the article.[1]  

Each of these can be turned into checks: verify proper presence/format when `og:type=article`.  
All: **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#type_article`

### 7.2 Book (`book`)

- **Check:** `og:type` is `book` for books.[1]  
  **Verifies:** Correct classification as a **book** object.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#type_book`

Type‑specific book properties:

- `book:author` – URL of one or more author profiles.[1]  
- `book:isbn` – International Standard Book Number.[1]  
- `book:release_date` – Date the book was released.[1]  
- `book:tag` – Tag words associated with the book.[1]  

**ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#type_book`

### 7.3 Profile (`profile`)

- **Check:** `og:type` is `profile` when the object describes a person’s profile.[1]  
  **Verifies:** Correct classification as a **profile**.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#type_profile`

Type‑specific profile properties:

- `profile:first_name` – First name.[1]  
- `profile:last_name` – Last name.[1]  
- `profile:username` – Username.[1]  
- `profile:gender` – Gender.[1]  

**ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#type_profile`

### 7.4 Music Types (`music:*`)

ogp.me defines several music object types:[1]

#### 7.4.1 `music.song`

- **Check:** `og:type` is `music.song`.[1]  
  **Verifies:** A **song** object.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#type_music.song`

Type‑specific properties include (examples):

- `music:duration` – Length of the song in seconds.[1]  
- `music:album` – URL(s) of the album(s) this song appears on; can have `music:album:disc` and `music:album:track` subproperties.[1]  
- `music:musician` – URL(s) of the musician(s).[1]  

#### 7.4.2 `music.album`

- **Check:** `og:type` is `music.album`.[1]  
  **Verifies:** A **music album** object.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#type_music.album`

Type‑specific properties (examples):

- `music:song` – URLs of songs on the album; each may have `music:song:disc` and `music:song:track` subproperties.[1]  
- `music:musician` – Musician(s) for the album.[1]  
- `music:release_date` – Album release date.[1]  

#### 7.4.3 `music.playlist`

- **Check:** `og:type` is `music.playlist`.[1]  
  **Verifies:** A **playlist** object.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#type_music.playlist`

Type‑specific properties:

- `music:song` – Song URLs; can have `music:song:disc` and `music:song:track` metadata.[1]  
- `music:creator` – Creator of the playlist.[1]  

#### 7.4.4 `music.radio_station`

- **Check:** `og:type` is `music.radio_station`.[1]  
  **Verifies:** A **radio station** object.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#type_music.radio_station`

Type‑specific properties (examples):

- `music:creator` – Creator/owner of the station.[1]  

(Exact property lists are in the ogp.me music type sections.)

### 7.5 Video Types (`video:*`)

ogp.me defines a generic `video.other` plus specific subtypes:[1]

#### 7.5.1 `video.movie`

- **Check:** `og:type` is `video.movie`.[1]  
  **Verifies:** A **movie** video object.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#type_video.movie`

Type‑specific properties:

- `video:actor` (with optional `video:actor:role`) – Actor URLs and roles.[1]  
- `video:director` – Director URLs.[1]  
- `video:writer` – Writer URLs.[1]  
- `video:duration` – Length in seconds.[1]  
- `video:release_date` – Release date.[1]  
- `video:tag` – Tag words.[1]  

#### 7.5.2 `video.episode`

- **Check:** `og:type` is `video.episode`.[1]  
  **Verifies:** A **TV episode** object.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#type_video.episode`

Type‑specific properties include those for actors, directors, writers, duration, release date, tag, plus:

- `video:series` – URL of the TV series this episode is part of.[1]  

#### 7.5.3 `video.tv_show`

- **Check:** `og:type` is `video.tv_show`.[1]  
  **Verifies:** A **TV show** object.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#type_video.tv_show`

Properties overlap with movie (actors, directors, etc.) as defined in the section.[1]

#### 7.5.4 `video.other`

- **Check:** `og:type` is `video.other` for videos that do not fit the other video types.[1]  
  **Verifies:** A generic **video** object.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#type_video.other`

---

## 8. Namespace / Prefix Usage

OGP relies on XML namespaces (RDFa‑style) for prefixes like `og:`, `article:`, `music:` etc.[1]

### 8.1 `og:` Namespace

- **Check:** Document declares the `og` namespace using RDFa `prefix` or an equivalent mechanism, for example:  
  `\<html prefix="og: https://ogp.me/ns#"\>`.[1][4]  
  **Verifies:** **`og` prefix** is properly bound to the OGP namespace.  
  **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#types` (RDFa example uses `prefix` attribute and `http://ogp.me/ns#`).

### 8.2 Type‑specific namespaces

When using type‑specific properties (e.g., `article:published_time`, `book:author`), the corresponding namespaces should be declared, for example:[1]

- **Check:** When using article properties, HTML declares something like:  
  `prefix="og: https://ogp.me/ns# article: https://ogp.me/ns/article#"`.[1]  
  **Verifies:** **`article` prefix** bound to the article namespace.  
  **ogp.me section:** Example under `https://ogp.me/#type_article`

Similarly for:

- `book` → `https://ogp.me/ns/book#` (used with book properties).[1]  
- `profile` → `https://ogp.me/ns/profile#`.[1]  
- `music` → `https://ogp.me/ns/music#`.[1]  
- `video` → `https://ogp.me/ns/video#`.[1]  

Each type section includes example RDFa prefix declarations you can use as the canonical reference.[1]

---

## 9. Additional Object Types (for completeness)

ogp.me lists many additional types (e.g., `website`, `product`, `business.business`, `place`, etc.) in the types section.[1][4]  
For testing, you can treat them as:

- **Check:** `og:type` value is one of the documented object types and, when type‑specific properties exist, the relevant namespace is declared as above.[1]  
- **Verifies:** Correct type usage and extensibility according to the OGP spec.  
- **ogp.me section:** `https://ogp.me/#types` (full type listing and namespace URIs).

---

If you want, I can turn this into a machine‑readable checklist (e.g., JSON schema‑like) tailored to an automated validator.
